EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.root_cause: DUPLICATE BATCH - 54,908 duplicate records in staging
EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.resolution: Deleted duplicate batch, journal now BALANCED
EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.data_integrity: iPOS RAW data was NEVER corrupted

STEP_1_RAW_VALIDATION.action: Extract and sum RAW tbl_accjurnal from iPOS backup

STEP_2_PARSED_VALIDATION.action: Compare parser output with RAW data

STEP_4_DUPLICATE_ANALYSIS.result: 54,908 records were inserted twice with same source_record_id

ROOT_CAUSE_ANALYSIS.explanation: During multiple extraction runs, a partial batch (f5ae1b60-...) was created with 54,908 duplicate journal entries. The staging service bulk insert did not prevent duplicates because each insert used a new staging_id.
ROOT_CAUSE_ANALYSIS.why_imbalanced: The duplicate 54,908 records had an internal imbalance of -Rp 2,246,550 because they were not a complete set of double-entry journals (partial extraction).

LESSONS_LEARNED: Staging process needs duplicate prevention by source_record_id
Batch management should prevent partial re-extractions
Always validate RAW source before assuming data corruption
Journal balance check should be run immediately after staging
RECOMMENDATIONS: Add unique index on (tenant_id, source_record_id) in staging collections
Add pre-import validation step to check for existing duplicates
Run journal balance check as part of extraction pipeline
